scenario: Lobsters work session - the shared vote/score recalculation (tight)

steps.step: Locate the recalculation and how its target is resolved

map_quality.rationale: Pins the call site at vote.rb:181 (update_score_and_recalculate!) and target resolution at vote.rb:185-191 (Comment.find vs Story.find), exactly the two anchors the rubric requires.

specificity.rationale: Both anchors are given as file:line ('app/models/vote.rb:181' and 'app/models/vote.rb:185-191'), with the resolving line 't = v.target' also pinned at vote.rb:177.

justification.rationale: Explains the dispatch via 'whichever of Comment/Story the vote points at' and names it as the shared entry point, but doesn't explicitly frame WHY the call site can't statically know which definition runs (polymorphic local).

uncertainty.rationale: No hedging or flagged hop; the answer is entirely declarative with no acknowledgement of unopened paths (e.g., the downstream calculated_* methods).

relationship.rationale: Surfaces that target resolves to one of Comment or Story based on comment_id, making the polymorphic discriminator that decides which definition runs explicit.

steps.step: Document what the recalculation recomputes and persists

map_quality.rationale: Pins both divergent definitions exactly: Comment at comment.rb:339-363 → calculated_confidence at comment.rb:232 writing score/flags/confidence/confidence_order, and Story at story.rb:693-718 → calculated_hotness at story.rb:550 writing score/flags/hotness. No conflation.

specificity.rationale: Every method and column is pinned with file:line (comment.rb:339, :232, :340-341; story.rb:693, :550, :694-695) and the persisted columns are enumerated explicitly per kind.

justification.rationale: Notes that confidence_order is packed from new_confidence + low byte of id and that Story re-sums flags excluding flags by users who commented, explaining the divergent semantics, though it doesn't spell out 'per-thread confidence vs front-page hotness' as the motivation.

uncertainty.rationale: Distinguishes in-memory bumps from the authoritative SQL re-aggregation, which is a calibrated hedge, but it doesn't explicitly flag any unopened hops or unverified column writes.

relationship.rationale: Each branch is correctly attributed to its own definition and cascade — Comment→calculated_confidence→confidence/confidence_order kept distinct from Story→calculated_hotness→hotness — with no conflation of the two same-named methods.

steps.step: Map the change and the specs that cover it

map_quality.rationale: Pins both files to edit (comment.rb:339-363, story.rb:693-718), the call site (vote.rb:181), value helpers (calculated_confidence:232, calculated_hotness:550), and concrete spec ranges across vote_spec, comment_spec, story_spec, and bitpacking_spec — exactly the regression net needed.

specificity.rationale: Files and specs are pinned with file:line ranges throughout (e.g., `spec/models/vote_spec.rb:15-81`, `spec/extras/bitpacking_spec.rb:156`, `app/models/comment.rb:339-363`) rather than vague 'add tests'.

justification.rationale: Each spec is annotated with what it covers (score/flags transitions, karma, flag-has-no-score-effect, confidence_order packing, flag aggregation), and the bitpacking spec is explicitly called out as 'the trickiest persisted column' — clear why each is in the map.

uncertainty.rationale: No explicit flag of what is least certain (e.g., whether other callers exhaustively cover all sites, or whether confidence_order packing has additional coverage); the answer reads as fully confident throughout.

relationship.rationale: Cleanly keeps each definition tied to its own computed value, columns, and spec — Comment → calculated_confidence → score/flags/confidence/confidence_order with bitpacking_spec; Story → calculated_hotness → score/flags/hotness with story_spec — no conflation.
